## [In tao::platform::windows](index.html)

# Trait WindowBuilderExtWindows Copy item path

`pub trait WindowBuilderExtWindows {
// Required methods
fn with_parent_window(self, parent: HWND) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_owner_window(self, parent: HWND) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_menu(self, menu: HMENU) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_taskbar_icon(self, taskbar_icon: Option<Icon>) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_no_redirection_bitmap(self, flag: bool) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_drag_and_drop(self, flag: bool) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_skip_taskbar(self, skip: bool) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_window_classname<S: Into<String>>(
self,
classname: S,
) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_undecorated_shadow(self, shadow: bool) -> WindowBuilder;
 fn with_rtl(self, rtl: bool) -> WindowBuilder;
}`

Additional methods on `WindowBuilder` that are specific to Windows.

`WindowBuilder`

## Required Methods[§](#required-methods)

#### fn [with\_parent\_window](#tymethod.with_parent_window)(self, parent: [HWND](type.HWND.html "type tao::platform::windows::HWND")) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Sets a parent to the window to be created.

A child window has the WS\_CHILD style and is confined to the client area of its parent window.

For more information, see <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winmsg/window-features#child-windows>

#### fn [with\_owner\_window](#tymethod.with_owner_window)(self, parent: [HWND](type.HWND.html "type tao::platform::windows::HWND")) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Set an owner to the window to be created. Can be used to create a dialog box, for example.
Can be used in combination with [`WindowExtWindows::set_enable(false)`](trait.WindowExtWindows.html#tymethod.set_enable "method tao::platform::windows::WindowExtWindows::set_enable")
on the owner window to create a modal dialog box.

`WindowExtWindows::set_enable(false)`

From MSDN:

For more information, see <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winmsg/window-features#owned-windows>

#### fn [with\_menu](#tymethod.with_menu)(self, menu: [HMENU](type.HMENU.html "type tao::platform::windows::HMENU")) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Sets a menu on the window to be created.

Parent and menu are mutually exclusive; a child window cannot have a menu!

The menu must have been manually created beforehand with [`windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::CreateMenu`]
or similar.

`windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::CreateMenu`

Note: Dark mode cannot be supported for win32 menus, it’s simply not possible to change how the menus look.
If you use this, it is recommended that you combine it with `with_theme(Some(Theme::Light))` to avoid a jarring effect.

`with_theme(Some(Theme::Light))`

#### fn [with\_taskbar\_icon](#tymethod.with_taskbar_icon)(self, taskbar\_icon: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")<[Icon](../../window/struct.Icon.html "struct tao::window::Icon")>) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

This sets `ICON_BIG`. A good ceiling here is 256x256.

`ICON_BIG`

#### fn [with\_no\_redirection\_bitmap](#tymethod.with_no_redirection_bitmap)(self, flag: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

This sets `WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP`.

`WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP`

#### fn [with\_drag\_and\_drop](#tymethod.with_drag_and_drop)(self, flag: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Enables or disables drag and drop support (enabled by default). Will interfere with other crates
that use multi-threaded COM API (`CoInitializeEx` with `COINIT_MULTITHREADED` instead of
`COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED`) on the same thread. Note that tao may still attempt to initialize
COM API regardless of this option. Currently only fullscreen mode does that, but there may be more in the future.
If you need COM API with `COINIT_MULTITHREADED` you must initialize it before calling any tao functions.
See <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/objbase/nf-objbase-coinitialize#remarks> for more information.

`CoInitializeEx`
`COINIT_MULTITHREADED`
`COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED`
`COINIT_MULTITHREADED`

#### fn [with\_skip\_taskbar](#tymethod.with_skip_taskbar)(self, skip: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Whether to create the window icon with the taskbar icon or not.

#### fn [with\_window\_classname](#tymethod.with_window_classname)<S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")<[String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")>>(self, classname: S) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Customize the window class name.

#### fn [with\_undecorated\_shadow](#tymethod.with_undecorated_shadow)(self, shadow: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Shows or hides the background drop shadow for undecorated windows.

The shadow is hidden by default.
Enabling the shadow causes a thin 1px line to appear on the top of the window.

#### fn [with\_rtl](#tymethod.with_rtl)(self, rtl: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [WindowBuilder](../../window/struct.WindowBuilder.html "struct tao::window::WindowBuilder")

Sets right-to-left layout.

## Dyn Compatibility[§](#dyn-compatibility)

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility).

*In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety".*
